things.name: Vieux-Lille
things.description: Charming old town of flamand baroque architecture with cobbled streets, trendy boutiques and outstanding restaurants

things.name: Palais des Beaux-Arts
things.description: France's second-greatest fine arts museum after the Louvre, with masterworks by Rubens, Van Dyck, Goya and Donatello

things.name: Grand Place (Place du Général de Gaulle)
things.description: Lille's magnificent central square dominated by the Goddess Column and surrounded by baroque Flemish-style buildings

things.name: Citadelle de Lille
things.description: Vauban's masterpiece star-shaped fortress of 1670, still in military use, surrounded by a moat and tree-lined boulevards

things.name: Musée de l'Hospice Comtesse
things.description: Beautifully preserved medieval hospital founded in 1237 now exhibiting Flemish furniture, paintings and devotional art

things.name: Euralille Shopping Centre
things.description: Vast futuristic shopping complex designed by Rem Koolhaas next to Lille-Europe station at the heart of the Eurostar hub

things.name: Place du Lion d'Or
things.description: Lively square in the heart of Vieux-Lille lined with terrace cafes ideal for watching city life over a Belgian beer

things.name: La Piscine Museum of Art and Industry
things.description: Extraordinary art deco swimming pool transformed into a museum of fine and applied arts in nearby Roubaix

things.name: Wazemmes Market
things.description: Huge, vibrant Sunday market in Lille's most multicultural neighbourhood, bursting with world foods and street music

things.name: Cathédrale Notre-Dame de la Treille
things.description: Unusual neo-Gothic cathedral with a stunning modern marble facade completed in 1999, glowing translucent gold at sunset
